meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Students: Project Management to Solve 'Stuck' Apps
content.seo_description: Learn how to master project management to overcome the 'stuck' phase of development. A beginner case study using Antigravity and Claude Code tools.
content.answer_block: To break through the 'tutorial hell' barrier, transition from writing isolated scripts to building structured project management systems. By leveraging Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you transform basic code into a cohesive, production-ready application, effectively bridging the gap between student projects and professional-grade software development.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks grinding through syntax, yet your portfolio remains a collection of disjointed scripts that don't talk to each other. The thrill of the first 'Hello World' has faded, replaced by the crushing frustration of a blank IDE and the feeling that you're just spinning your wheels in a loop of endless tutorials.

But there is a bridge between being a student and becoming an engineer. The secret isn't just writing more code; it is mastering the architecture of project management. By treating your code like a professional product, you stop building 'things' and start building solutions. Let’s dismantle that wall and turn your next idea into a functional, scalable reality.

steps.title: Define the MVP Scope
steps.description: Stop trying to build the next Facebook. Focus on a single, high-value feature that solves a specific user pain point.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Lead Architect. Create a minimal scope document for a personal task management app. Include the core data model and three primary user stories.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Initialize a project structure for a task manager with a README, src/ folder, and a basic requirements.md file.'

steps.title: Architect the Database Schema
steps.description: A solid app starts with a solid data structure. Define your relationships before writing a single line of business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a relational database schema for a task management system. Include Users, Tasks, and Categories tables with appropriate foreign keys.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate a schema.sql file based on the task management requirements and define the relationship constraints.'

steps.title: Implement the API Layer
steps.description: Build the bridge between your data and your UI. Focus on clean, RESTful endpoints.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a boilerplate for a Node.js Express API that handles CRUD operations for the task management system.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create controller and route files for Task CRUD operations. Ensure standard error handling is included.'

steps.title: Iterative Frontend Integration
steps.description: Connect your logic to a UI. Use a component-based approach to keep your code maintainable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a React component structure for a task dashboard that fetches data from the API created in step 3.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate the TaskList and TaskItem components using Tailwind CSS for styling.'

steps.title: Automated Testing & Refinement
steps.description: Professional code is tested code. Use Claude to write unit tests for your core logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of Jest unit tests for the task management business logic, covering edge cases like empty inputs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Run the test suite and fix any identified logic gaps in the task controller.'

pro_tips: Always build your 'Data Access Layer' before your UI to ensure your app is modular and testable.
Use 'Feature Flags' to toggle experimental features, preventing the 'stuck' feeling when a new addition breaks existing code.
Document your API endpoints using OpenApi/Swagger; it forces you to think like a backend engineer.
Never commit code that hasn't been linted; it builds the discipline required for professional team environments.
Treat your README file as a living document; if you can't explain the feature in the README, you haven't simplified it enough.
content.next_steps: Once your app is functional, deploy it to a platform like Vercel or Railway and share it with the community to get real-world feedback.
content.conversion_block: Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
